chunk_summary: Guidance on managing discordant ALT and ultrasound findings in pediatric MASLD screening, emphasizing risk-based approaches, lifestyle interventions, and appropriate follow-up strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the limitations of ultrasound and ALT in pediatric MASLD diagnosis.
Learn practical management strategies for discordant ALT and ultrasound findings.
Identify metabolic risk factors and their role in MASLD progression.
Apply NASPGHAN guidelines to optimize screening and follow-up.
key_facts: Ultrasound detects moderate-to-severe steatosis but is unreliable for mild steatosis or quantification.
ALT values below 40 can occur in children with imaging steatosis; persistently elevated ALT with normal ultrasound warrants MASLD exclusion work-up.
NASPGHAN discourages routine ultrasound for screening due to limited sensitivity and specificity.
Reassess metabolic drivers like waist trajectory, fasting glucose, triglycerides, and BP when discordance occurs.
Repeat ALT in 6–12 months or sooner if weight gain, diabetes, or rising triglycerides are present.
reference_quote: The 2017 NASPGHAN guidance specifically discourages routine ultrasound as a screening test because sensitivity and specificity are limited; a normal ultrasound does not exclude steatosis, and an abnormal ultrasound does not prove MASLD.
document_key_topics: Discordance between ALT and ultrasound findings in MASLD screening
Limitations of ultrasound and ALT in pediatric MASLD diagnosis
Risk-based management and follow-up strategies for MASLD
